title: Multiscale modeling of materials and neural operators
abstract: Multiscale modeling is essential for understanding the complex behavior of materials. However, accurately transferring all relevant information from one scale to another has remained an outstanding challenge. Neural operators, discretization-independent generalizations of neural networks, is proving to be a powerful tool in addressing this challenge. This article provides an introduction to neural operators, and illustrates their use in multiscale modeling of materials through three selected examples.
summary: This paper introduces neural operators as a method for multiscale modeling in materials science. It highlights how these discretization-independent neural networks can help bridge the gap in information transfer between different physical scales.
main_problem: The difficulty of accurately transferring relevant information between different length scales in multiscale modeling of materials.
main_result: Demonstrates that neural operators, which are discretization-independent neural networks, serve as a powerful tool for addressing multiscale modeling challenges.
method: Introduction and application of neural operators, which are generalizations of neural networks that are independent of discretization.
model_or_system: The paper focuses on materials science applications involving multiscale modeling, illustrated through three selected examples.
paper_structure: The paper provides an introduction to the concept of neural operators followed by three specific examples of their application in materials multiscale modeling.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
